mechanism_narrative: The Federal Reserve's aggressive easing to near-zero interest rates compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS dropped to 0.39% from 4.5% in early 2008
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 1% and decreasing.

mechanism_narrative: The rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 6% signals severe labor market weakness, which reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increase of more than 1 percentage point over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE rose from 5.0% to 6.7% in 6 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE increases by >1pp over 6 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in at least 60% of such periods, or the mean return is not significantly below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in real personal consumption expenditures indicate weakening consumer demand, which directly lowers corporate revenues and profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS fell from $365B to $355B in recent months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns conditional upon RSAFS decline >0.5% MoM are not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production signal a shrinking supply side of the economy, reducing overall output and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO fell from 109 to 106 in recent months
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines >0.5% MoM, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not negative in the majority of observations (p<0.10 sign test).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero interest rates and collapsing housing starts indicates that monetary policy is ineffective in stimulating housing, reflecting a severe credit crunch that deeply weighs on equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts plunged to 791 thousand units, lowest on record
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS below 1% and HOUST below 1000, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (p<0.10 one-tailed), or the interaction term in a regression is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent declines in retail sales throughout 2008 signal a sustained deterioration in consumer spending, which continuously depresses corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 returns even over short 5-day horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS has fallen for several consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns during the period of declining RSAFS are not significantly negative (p<0.10 one-sided t-test).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is above 6% and rising rapidly, historical patterns in severe recessions suggest that oversold conditions lead to short-term bounces, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: doc:Historical bounce patterns in deep recessions
failure_condition: If UNRATE >6% and rising >0.5pp per month, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the mean return is not significantly positive (p<0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Deflationary signals from declining consumer prices erode nominal revenues and increase real debt burdens, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI declined in October 2008 for the first time in decades
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL declines >0.1% MoM, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (p<0.10 one-tailed).
